_README: Frozen Claude courtroom + self-critique transcript for the demo, on a REFUSED (KILL) case. The live / last_call_live flags below are taken verbatim from the Claude client badge: with no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY the deterministic OFFLINE TEMPLATE produced this text (live=false). Rerun with a key present to capture a genuine live-Claude transcript — this generator NEVER stamps live=true over template output.

claim.claim_text: A structural embedding signature separates MCI converters from non-converters.

courtroom.prosecution: The claim that frozen Neuro-JEPA structural embeddings separate MCI converters from MCI non-converters does not survive scrutiny. The subject-disjoint leakage margin is only -0.08 AUC — the same head predicts the scanner nearly as well as the outcome, exactly the batch-effect mechanic reported in 'Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings' (arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)). It failed or weakened under: Age / sex adjustment (failed), Site / scanner leakage (failed), Biomarker anchor (failed). The parsimonious reading is an acquisition/aging artifact until an orthogonal molecular anchor says otherwise.
courtroom.defense: The signal separating MCI converters from MCI non-converters is consistent with real disease biology. It withstood: Brain-age control, Replication split. The molecular anchor is incomplete, so the defense rests on the imaging survivors alone and asks only for follow-up, not acceptance.
courtroom.judge_reasoning: Robustness score 40/100 places this finding at 'partially robust'. The -0.08 AUC leakage margin is the deciding fact: the prosecution's artifact reading is the more defensible one. The finding sits below the promotion floor; it is not carried forward to a mechanism, and would need a stronger leakage margin or molecular anchor to be worth a scientist's quarter.

critique: ‘Partially robust’ is not ‘robust’. The score renormalizes over the tests that ran, so a middling verdict can hide a dimension that quietly failed — read the per-test results, not just the headline.

The brain-age control is a proxy, not a gold standard: it is embedding-derived (this cohort's fit: R2=0.87, MAE=1.6yr), so residual generic-aging signal can survive the adjustment and masquerade as disease-specific.
The biomarker anchor likely rests on a partial complete-case subset; with realistic p-tau217 missingness the correlation is estimated on far fewer subjects than the headline cohort.
N is small (~137); at this size AUCs are noisy and a single site's idiosyncrasy can dominate — treat effect sizes as wide-interval estimates.

The leakage test audits the embeddings with the same linear-probe family it uses for the outcome, so it bounds — but cannot fully rule out — shared confounding; this is the mechanic quantified in ‘Pretrained, Frozen, Still Leaking: Auditing Cross-Encoder Attribute Transfer in EEG Foundation Models’ (arXiv:2606.09189 (2026)), which we cite rather than claim.
revised_caveats: Verdict is provisional and conditional on the scanner/site distribution of any replication cohort.
Brain-age adjustment uses a proxy control; residual aging signal cannot be excluded.
Any biomarker anchor is complete-case only and may not generalize.
Leakage is bounded, not eliminated — see the published batch-effect audits (‘Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,’ arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)).

narration: The claim “A structural embedding signature separates MCI converters from non-converters.” lands at “partially robust” (40/100). The naive effect (AUC 0.87 separating MCI converters from MCI non-converters) is real on paper, but the referee's job is to ask what else could produce it. It sits below the promotion floor, so the honest read is that acquisition, demographics or generic aging can plausibly account for it, and it should not consume a scientist's quarter without stronger evidence. Where it stops being true: that the scanner/site mix in any new cohort matches this one — the leakage margin is only -0.08 AUC, so a different scanner distribution could erase the effect. That the same frozen embeddings can predict scanner/site as well as outcome is documented prior art (“Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,” arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)); NeuroAD Discovery Engine only runs the audit and issues the verdict.
